description: My brother first observed the bird skulking in dense undergrowth along a thickly-vegetated ditch (mostly russian-olives). It came into view for a few seconds in response to his pishing before it disappeared. He immediately identified it as a male Mourning Warbler due to the complete lack of white eye arcs. We spent the next 15 minutes trying to locate it without success. Then, as I was heading out to the road, I located it foraging low to the ground in deciduous woods about 20 feet from where it was initially observed. I was able to get several good views in decent light, but I couldn't obtain any pictures. This was a medium-sized, chunky warbler with lemon-yellow underparts and a greenish back. It had a dark sooty/slate gray hood with a noticeably blacker patch at the bottom of the chest. Its head was uniform gray without any hint of an eyering.
